memes.label: Church and Religion
memes.description: "The role of the Church of England in rural life in The American Senator — an institution whose spiritual authority is inseparable from its social function as part of the landed gentry's apparatus of order"

memes.label: Courtship
memes.description: "The social and economic institution of courtship in The American Senator, where marriage negotiations reveal the intersection of love, money, class ambition, and family strategy in Victorian England"

memes.label: Dillsborough
memes.description: The fictional market town in Anthony Trollope's The American Senator that serves as the novel's primary rural setting, located in hunting country between the estates of Bragton Park and Rufford Hall.

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: "The central moral concept in The American Senator — a word invoked by characters across the social spectrum to justify, excuse, or enforce behavior, revealing Trollope's anatomy of Victorian moral reasoning"

memes.label: England and America
memes.description: "The transatlantic theme at the heart of The American Senator — Trollope's use of the English-American contrast to anatomize both societies through mutual observation and critique"

memes.label: English Society
memes.description: "The hierarchical social structure of rural England as depicted in The American Senator, encompassing class, rank, obligation, and the moral economy of country life"

memes.label: Field Sports
memes.description: "The system of field sports in The American Senator — shooting, game preservation, and the conflicts between sporting rights, property rights, and agricultural livelihood in Trollope's rural England"

memes.label: The Foreign Office
memes.description: "The government department in The American Senator where John Morton works — representing metropolitan careerism, diplomatic ambition, and the professional path that distances him from his Dillsborough inheritance"

memes.label: The Goarly Affair
memes.description: "The legal dispute over a poisoned fox in Dillsborough Wood that crystallizes the novel's central conflicts — hunting rights versus property rights, aristocratic power versus rural grievance, and English custom versus American critique"

memes.label: House of Commons
memes.description: "The elected chamber of Parliament in The American Senator — a subject of Senator Gotobed's sustained critique and a symbol of English constitutional arrangements that the novel examines through transatlantic comparison"

memes.label: Fox-Hunting
memes.description: "The central social ritual of Rufford county life in The American Senator, structuring class hierarchy, community identity, and English rural tradition"

memes.label: Hunting Meets
memes.description: "The regular gatherings of the U.R.U. Hunt that serve as the primary social ritual of Rufford county — where class hierarchy is performed, reaffirmed, and occasionally challenged in The American Senator"

memes.label: Inheritance
memes.description: "The transmission of property and status across generations in The American Senator — the legal, moral, and psychological dimensions of inheritance as a central mechanism of English social order"

memes.label: John Morton
memes.description: "The returning squire of Bragton — a man caught between his American experience and English inheritance, between Arabella Trefoil's calculation and his own unexamined assumptions about class, duty, and belonging"

memes.label: Justice and Law
memes.description: "The legal system and the pursuit of justice in The American Senator — Trollope's examination of how English law serves property, the class structure, and the professional interests of lawyers rather than abstract ideals of fairness"

memes.label: Kate Masters
memes.description: "The younger daughter of the Dillsborough attorney — a 'jolly bouncing schoolgirl' whose cheerful presence on the hunting field and in the Masters household provides a lighter counterpoint to her half-sister Mary's more complicated romantic situation"

memes.label: Lady Augustus Trefoil
memes.description: "Arabella Trefoil's mother and chief strategist — a titled woman whose own disappointing marriage has made her the marriage market's most cynical and effective operator"

memes.label: Lady Penwether
memes.description: "Lord Rufford's sister and hostess at Rufford Hall, whose social authority as the great man's gatekeeper makes her a pivotal figure in Arabella Trefoil's marital campaign"

memes.label: Lady Ushant
memes.description: "The 'honourable old lady' of Bragton whose settled moral authority makes her the novel's ethical touchstone — beloved by all Mortons, protector of Mary Masters, and embodiment of English gentility at its finest"

memes.label: Lawrence Twentyman
memes.description: "The gentleman-farmer of Dillsborough whose honest but socially anxious pursuit of Mary Masters exposes the class barriers that no amount of land or hunt subscriptions can overcome"

memes.label: London
memes.description: "The metropolitan counterpoint to Dillsborough in The American Senator — site of the Foreign Office, the marriage-market's highest stakes, and the social world that country gentry both disdain and depend upon"

memes.label: Lord Mistletoe
memes.description: "A minor aristocratic figure known for his parliamentary erudition and tobacco connoisseurship — Trollope's comic sketch of hereditary political authority reduced to a hobby"

memes.label: Lord Rufford
memes.description: "The greatest landowner in Rufford county — a wealthy peer whose dominance over the hunt, the county, and the marriage market illustrates the unexamined power of English aristocracy"

memes.label: Manners and Etiquette
memes.description: "The unwritten codes of social conduct in The American Senator — how manners function as markers of class, instruments of exclusion, and mechanisms of social control in Trollope's rural England"

memes.label: The Marriage Market
memes.description: "The transactional logic governing matrimonial negotiations in The American Senator, where love is subordinate to financial settlement, family strategy, and class ambition"

memes.label: Mary Masters
memes.description: "The attorney's daughter caught between the respectable suit of Lawrence Twentyman and her deeper feeling for Reginald Morton — a quiet heroine whose moral seriousness anchors the novel's romantic plot"

memes.label: Mr. Goarly
memes.description: "The tenant farmer accused of poisoning a fox on Lord Rufford's land — a figure whose persecution by the hunt community exposes the class violence latent in English rural solidarity"

memes.label: Mr. Masters
memes.description: "The Dillsborough attorney whose professional respectability and social anxiety place him at the boundary of gentility — a man who knows the rules of class distinction because he lives at their edge"

memes.label: Mr. Runciman
memes.description: "The landlord of the Bush Inn at Dillsborough — a man of trade whose commercial shrewdness and social observation make him the novel's most reliable commentator on the county's class dynamics"

memes.label: Mrs. Masters
memes.description: "The attorney's second wife whose domestic tyranny and social anxiety drive the Masters household — a woman whose shrewishness is the product of a class position that offers respectability without security"

memes.label: Property and Land
memes.description: "The economic foundation of English society in The American Senator — how the ownership of land determines social position, political power, and personal identity in Trollope's Dillsborough"

memes.label: Reginald Morton
memes.description: "The younger half-uncle of John Morton — an Oxford-educated gentleman of quiet integrity whose love for Mary Masters provides the novel's most genuine romantic resolution"

memes.label: Reputation and Honour
memes.description: "The moral currency of reputation in The American Senator, governing conduct across all social classes from the honourable dowager to the suspected poisoner — a code that is as much about public perception as private conscience"

memes.label: Senator Gotobed
memes.description: "The American senator from Mickewa whose visit to England provides Trollope's novel with its transatlantic lens — an honest, inquisitive, and often bewildered observer of English social customs and class hierarchy"

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: "The layered hierarchy of English rural society in The American Senator, where class identity determines access, marriage prospects, and moral standing in Dillsborough and Rufford county"

memes.label: The American Senator (novel)
memes.description: "Anthony Trollope's 1877 novel of English rural society and Anglo-American encounter — the eighty-first of his forty-seven novels, set in the fictional hunting country of Dillsborough"
